title: Intel Xeon 6+ Computex roundtable interview transcript Kira Boyko and Tim Wilson on 18A wafer allocation, Clearwater Forest, and dropping hyper-threading
summary: Intel's Xeon 6+ processors were launched at Computex, sparking a roundtable discussion. Kira Boyko and Tim Wilson, key figures behind the product, addressed pressing questions from the press. Topics included 18A wafer allocation, Clearwater Forest, and the decision to drop hyper-threading.
insight: Intel's Xeon 6+ marks a strategic shift in high-performance computing.

title: Bernie Sanders pushes for 50% public ownership of American AI companies proposes AI sovereign wealth fund that would hold direct ownership stakes in largest AI firms
summary: Senator Bernie Sanders proposes 50% public ownership of AI companies, citing public data revenue as a reason for the public to benefit. He suggests establishing an AI sovereign wealth fund to hold direct stakes in major AI firms. This move aims to give the public a say in AI development.
insight: Public benefit from data-driven revenue generation is a core issue.

title: Gigabyte showcases new Infinity products for its 40th anniversary X870 Infinity Next halo motherboard boasts metal 3D-printed elements, Aero Wood goes dark, MicroATX Stealth boards, Infinity-style GPUs extend down the product stack
summary: Gigabyte celebrates 40 years with new Infinity products, including a Next halo motherboard and Aero Wood design refresh. The X870 Infinity Next motherboard features metal 3D-printed elements. Gigabyte also introduces extended Infinity-style GPUs and MicroATX Stealth boards.
insight: Gigabyte's 40th anniversary marks a significant product design refresh.

title: SK hynix to double memory wafer capacity within five years, chairman says AI-driven shortage will persist until at least 2030
summary: SK hynix plans to double its memory wafer capacity within five years, with chairman Chey Tae-won citing the ongoing AI-driven semiconductor shortage as a persisting issue until at least 2030.
insight: AI-driven shortage to persist for over a decade ahead

title: Jensen Huang says Nvidia wants to 'reinvent the single most important tool of humanity' with RTX Spark Nvidia CEO touts support of 'literally every computer maker in the world' for its agentic AI PC platform
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang discusses Nvidia's ambitions for the future of computing, including its entry into the PC market with the RTX Spark platform, which aims to 'reinvent the single most important tool of humanity' with agentic AI support.
insight: Nvidia seeks to revolutionize computing with AI-powered PC innovation.

title: Tom's Hardware Unfiltered: Computex 2026, Day 1 night markets, taking the MRT train, and a slew of demos
summary: The Tom's Hardware team shares their experience at Computex 2026, covering night markets, MRT trains, and demos of the latest hardware innovations.
insight: Computex 2026 showcases cutting-edge hardware amidst vibrant Taiwanese culture.

title: Drones to protect undersea cables from Russian sabotage touted in new defense pact US one of three partners developing new tech to protect $1.8 trillion in daily transactions
summary: The US, UK, and Australia join forces to protect undersea cables from sabotage. The partnership aims to develop new technologies, including drones, to safeguard these vital communication routes. This move comes in response to recent incidents and Russian Navy's surveying activities.
insight: Protection of $1.8 trillion daily transactions relies on secure undersea cables

title: AMD had to re-engineer the Ryzen 7 5800X3D for a re-release 10th Anniversary Edition chip had a whole body of engineering work put into it
summary: AMD had to re-engineer the Ryzen 7 5800X3D due to a change in fabrication method, requiring a significant investment of engineering work to release a 10th Anniversary Edition. The original manufacturing process was no longer viable due to a new TSMC bonding method, necessitating a complete overhaul. This re-engineering effort involved substantial redesign and testing.
insight: Manufacturing process changes require continuous innovation and re-engineering.

title: Intel Arc G3 interview transcript Intel's Senior Product Director talks new handheld chips, Arrow Lake Refresh, and RTX Spark
summary: Intel's Nish Neelalojanan discusses the G3 chip line's impact on handheld gaming market, and how it addresses rising chip and memory prices.
insight: Intel enters handheld gaming with competitive, affordable chips and solutions

title: Frore System s solid-state AirJet Mini cools Intel s Wildcat Lake laptop reference design 15W of sustained, fanless cooling helps MacBook Neo competitor reach a svelte 11.3 mm, remain silent
summary: Frore's AirJet solid-state cooling chip is featured in Intel's Wildcat Lake laptop reference design, enabling 15W of sustained, fanless cooling and a slim 11.3mm profile.
insight: Intel gains competitive edge with silent, fanless cooling technology.

title: Teen s Bluetooth speaker named BOMB caused a 10-hour delay on flight from Newark to Spain passenger reported concerns to flight attendant at 32,000 feet, forcing plane back to the US
summary: A United Airlines flight from Newark to Spain was delayed 10 hours due to a Bluetooth speaker named 'BOMB,' causing a security concern at 32,000 feet.
insight: Sensitizing technology names can cause unnecessary disruptions and delays.

title: Intel says 'something has to give' with memory prices company says it 'will continue to make sure that there are products which can take care of older memory technologies'
summary: Intel acknowledges the importance of Raptor Lake and DDR4 platforms in addressing the ongoing memory crunch. The company aims to support older memory technologies through its products. This move is seen as a strategic response to the current market conditions.
insight: Intel recognizes Raptor Lake's relevance in a memory-constrained market.

title: Chinese military has been acquiring Nvidia chips, even post-Washington export controls, research claims multiple institutions linked to the PLA asked for Nvidia AI chips, according to publicly available documents
summary: The Chinese military has been acquiring Nvidia AI chips despite US export controls, according to publicly available documents. Multiple institutions linked to the PLA have requested these chips by name or specifications. This suggests a continued attempt to circumvent restrictions.
insight: Circumventing export controls poses significant national security risks globally.

title: Watch Intel's Computex 2026 keynote here CEO Lip-Bu Tan takes the stage in Taipei at 10:30pm PT on June 1
summary: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan will deliver a keynote address at Computex 2026, taking place at 10:30 pm PT on June 1 in Taipei.
insight: Intel to showcase upcoming innovative technologies at Computex 2026.

title: Asus shows off updated Zenbook and Strix Scar laptops, along with a TUF-based Gaming desktop a refreshed look on laptops takes center stage
summary: Asus unveiled updated Zenbook, Expertbook, and Strix Scar laptops at Computex 2026, along with the TUF T700 gaming desktop. The event highlighted a refreshed design and new features across the lineup. This marks a significant update to Asus' gaming and productivity laptops.
insight: Innovative design enhances user experience in Asus' latest laptop lineup.

title: Hands-on with Asus ROG Azoth Extreme Edition 20 mechanical keyboard when carbon fiber isn t enough, Asus goes for the gold
summary: Asus ROG Azoth Extreme Edition 20 is a high-end gaming keyboard with gold-plated accents, 8K polling, transparent switches and keycaps, and an OLED touchscreen, priced at $599.
insight: Luxury gaming keyboard design pushes boundaries of premium gaming experience

title: Hands-on with Asus ROG Harpe II Extreme Edition 20 gaming mouse 24K gold and a 65K sensor
summary: Asus ROG Harpe II Extreme Edition 20 is a limited-edition gaming mouse that celebrates the brand's 20-year milestone, featuring a luxurious 24K gold and black design, along with a see-through backside. This high-end gaming peripheral boasts an impressive 65K sensor for precise tracking and accurate performance. The ROG Harpe II is a premium offering for serious gamers seeking top-notch gaming experience.
insight: Luxurious design meets high-performance gaming with cutting-edge tech specs.

title: AMD Radeon RX 9070 GRE review: thoroughly midrange
summary: The AMD Radeon RX 9070 GRE offers strong high-refresh-rate gaming at 1080p and 1440p, making it a viable option for midrange users. Although its performance is solid, the price tag of $549 is somewhat high for a midrange GPU. Overall, the RX 9070 GRE is a thoroughly midrange graphics card.
insight: Price holds back RX 9070 GRE from midrange dominance potential

title: Gigabyte debuts fourth-gen Tandem WOLED and multi-mode Mini LED gaming monitors 27 to 32 inches, up to 480 Hz, and up to 5K resolution
summary: Gigabyte debuts fourth-gen Tandem WOLED gaming monitors with high refresh rates and resolutions.
insight: Enhanced gaming experiences with high-end visuals and ultra-fast refresh rates.

title: Noctua announces new thermal pad for AMD chips in partnership with Carbice product will work with processors in AM5 and AM4 sockets
summary: Noctua has partnered with Carbice to release a new thermal pad, NT-CP1, designed for AMD Ryzen CPUs in both AM5 and AM4 sockets.
insight: Enhanced cooling solutions for AMD users with improved thermal performance.

title: Windows Server vulnerability can grant system privileges with just a malformed packet domain controllers are being exploited in the wild
summary: A high-severity Windows Server vulnerability (9.8-rated) can grant system privileges through a malformed packet. This allows attackers to exploit domain controllers in the wild. The vulnerability poses a significant threat to network security.
insight: Unpatched domain controllers offer unsecured backdoors to attackers.

title: Anthropic files for IPO Claude maker races OpenAI and SpaceX to Wall Street
summary: Anthropic confidentially filed to go public with the SEC, competing with OpenAI and SpaceX.
insight: Anthropic's Claude AI tech poses a significant AI industry disruption threat.

title: Computex 2026 Day Zero Wrap-Up: Nvidia launches RTX Spark Superchip assault on laptop and desktop markets, Intel readies Xeon 6+
summary: Nvidia unveiled the RTX Spark Superchip, a powerful GPU aimed at laptops and desktops. Meanwhile, Intel announced the Xeon 6+, a new line of processors. These announcements mark a significant push in the tech industry's competition for market dominance.
insight: High-performance computing just got a major competitive upgrade

title: Nvidia's RTX Spark could caplitalize where Qualcomm's Arm-based efforts have not following the expiration of Qualcomm's Windows on Arm deal, Nvidia stands poised to pick up the slack
summary: Nvidia's RTX Spark superchip combines a 20-core Arm-based CPU with a Blackwell RTX GPU, marking a key step in the company's Arm-based efforts. This move comes as Qualcomm's Windows on Arm deal has expired, leaving room for Nvidia to capitalize. The RTX Spark superchip is set to be showcased at Nvidia's GTC Taipei event.
insight: Nvidia fills void left by Qualcomm's expired Windows on Arm deal

title: Qualcomm aims Snapdragon C laptop chip at the budget laptop segment, as manufacturers feel the DRAM squeeze analysts warn sub $500 laptop market may disappear before 2028
summary: Qualcomm announced the Snapdragon C Platform on May 28th, ahead of Computex 2026 in Taipei.

title: Asus' world-first OLED esports monitor can hit 540Hz at 1080p ROG Strix OLED model among four fresh offerings
summary: Asus releases four new ROG monitors with OLED and Fast IPS panels, featuring high refresh rates, including the world's first 540Hz 1080p OLED esports monitor, the ROG Strix OLED.
insight: High refresh rates enhance gaming performance and visual experience.

title: Wireless-Tag ESP32P4C61-TINY board combines ESP32-P4 and ESP32-C61 SoCs (Crowdfunding)
summary: Wireless-Tag's ESP32P4C61-TINY is a compact AIoT board combining ESP32-P4 and ESP32-C61 SoCs for edge computing and AI applications. It features MIPI CSI and DSI interfaces, a microSD card slot, and supports camera and display connectivity. This board is suitable for various AIoT and edge computing use cases.
insight: Combining AI and wireless capabilities in a tiny, open-source package.

title: Nordic adds AI-assisted development to the nRF Connect SDK and nRF Cloud
summary: Nordic Semiconductor has integrated AI-assisted development into its nRF Connect SDK and nRF Cloud, providing developers with access to specific, validated resources and integrating with their favorite IDE and AI assistants at a lower cost.
insight: Nordic's AI-assisted development provides tailored support for specific IoT applications.

title: ASUS Ascent QN10 mini PC is powered by Qualcomm Snapdragon X2 Elite 18-core Armv9 processor
summary: ASUS Ascent QN10 is a powerful mini PC featuring a 18-core Armv9 processor and 80 TOPS AI performance for seamless Windows CoPilot+ experience. It comes with up to 32GB LPDDR5 memory and 512GB to 2TB NVMe SSD storage. The mini PC also supports 2.5GbE and WiFi 7 networking.
insight: Breakthrough in AI performance for edge computing devices

title: Sharpa Wave is a high-end dexterous robotic hand with 22 DoF, high-sensitivity dynamic tactile array
summary: The Sharpa Wave is a high-end dexterous robotic hand with 22 DoF and a dynamic tactile array, designed to mimic human form and manipulation capabilities, enabling detection of lightweight objects.
insight: Advancements in robotic hands could revolutionize industrial and service sectors.

title: Robot maker Trossen sees hottest market in decades
summary: Trossen Robotics demonstrated advanced robot arms at Computex, powered by Intel Core Ultra processors and trained with AI, pointing to a significant growth in the robotics market.
insight: AI-powered robots drive innovation and growth in the robotics industry

title: Former Tesla employees say full self-driving is nowhere near as capable as Elon Musk claims
summary: Former Tesla employees claim the Full Self-Driving (FSD) system is not as advanced as Elon Musk suggests, citing frequent mistakes during data-labeling reviews. The employees, who worked in a Utah data-labeling office, stated that the system often failed to handle critical driving situations. These reviews involve hundreds of workers who tag and analyze footage from Tesla vehicles.
insight: Tesla's FSD system has significant limitations and safety concerns

title: AMD Radeon RX 9070 GRE Review: An Awkward Addition to the Lineup
summary: The AMD Radeon RX 9070 GRE, a year-old GPU, faces challenges in its market due to its high price and crowded competition.
insight: Outdated GPU struggles in crowded market with high price tag.

title: Craigslist founder and Sesame Street's Count von Count team up to fight online scams
summary: Craigslist founder Craig Newmark and Count von Count team up to combat online scams through Take9 campaign, promoting habits for safe online browsing.
insight: Combating online scams is crucial for protecting vulnerable internet users worldwide.

title: Anthropic files for IPO, joining OpenAI and SpaceX in the race to go public
summary: Anthropic has begun the IPO process, submitting a draft registration statement. The company's offering details remain undisclosed, pending market conditions. Anthropic joins OpenAI and SpaceX in pursuing a public listing.
insight: AI companies are rapidly expanding into public markets.

title: elementary OS 8.1 focuses on polish, security, and a smoother Linux desktop
summary: elementary OS 8.1 focuses on polish, security, and a smoother Linux desktop experience with improvements in Wayland, multitasking, and hardware compatibility.
insight: Enhanced security boosts Linux user adoption and trust.

title: Asus unveils 3,000W PSU that can power up to four GeForce RTX 5090 GPUs
summary: Asus has unveiled a 3,000W PSU that can power up to four GeForce RTX 5090 GPUs, part of the ROG brand's celebration. The ROG Thor 3,000W Titanium III Edition 20 is designed for gaming-focused hardware. This PSU aims to cater to high-performance systems.
insight: Unprecedented power for extreme gaming and high-end PC builds

title: Meta's AI training effort is capturing employee emails and browsing history, not just mouse clicks
summary: Meta's AI training tool, MCI, collects employee data from over 200 apps and websites, raising privacy concerns.
insight: Massive data collection poses significant risks to employee privacy and trust.

title: Intel Crescent Island data center GPU supports up to 480GB LPDDR5X with 350W air-cooled TDP
summary: Intel's Crescent Island GPU uses LPDDR5X DRAM for AI workloads, air-cooled design.
insight: Intel offers affordable AI acceleration with consumer-grade memory technology.

title: Zig project leader says AI-generated code contributions are "invariably garbage" and will be rejected
summary: Zig project leader rejects AI-generated code contributions as 'invariably garbage'.
insight: Trust in AI coding abilities affects open-source project contribution standards.

title: Dell's new XPS 13 starts at $599 and is gunning directly for the MacBook Neo
summary: Dell launches XPS 13 at $599, targeting MacBook Neo with promotional price.
insight: Dell challenges Apple's dominance in budget ultrabook market space.

title: A top economist says there's "zero evidence" AI is killing jobs, despite thousands of actual layoffs
summary: A top economist claims there's no evidence AI is causing job losses, citing ADP employment data that shows private companies added nearly 110,000 jobs in April.
insight: Economist's conclusion may contradict recent mass layoffs attributed to AI adoption.

title: This solar-powered desalination device turns seawater into drinking water, can also extract lithium
summary: A solar-powered desalination device uses superwicking black metal to turn seawater into drinking water and extract lithium.
insight: Revolutionary material could provide clean drinking water and energy resources globally.

title: Microsoft unveils Surface Laptop Ultra with Nvidia RTX Spark chip and up to 128GB of RAM
summary: Microsoft unveils Surface Laptop Ultra with Nvidia RTX Spark chip, featuring up to 128GB of RAM and a standard design.
insight: Groundbreaking performance in a compact, user-friendly laptop design.
